description: Set up autoresearch harness and recorded baseline render benchmark
Measured baseline noise floor without code changes
Tried Output fast-path branches for string targets to avoid trait dispatch in write_str/write_char
Optimize upper filter with ASCII no-op fast path to avoid redundant case conversion
Tried ASCII-specific to_ascii_uppercase path in upper filter for lowercase ASCII strings
Add html-escape precheck and direct write_str fast path for strings without escapable chars
Specialized HTML formatter for primitive ValueRepr variants to bypass Value Display dispatch
Attempted to force-inline write_with_html_escaping in utils hot path
Tried simpler any(matches!) implementation for needs_html_escaping byte scan
Control run without code changes to re-check variance after recent edits
Second control run; observed benchmark noise drifting higher on current machine load
Bypass formatter callback for default formatter using formatter_is_default fast path
Tried force-inlining Environment::format after adding default formatter fast path
Rewrote undefined-check match in Environment::format as early-return if branch
Forced inline on get_or_lookup_local cache helper in VM
Added aggressive inlining hints to VM stack push/pop and call-arg helpers
Specialize VM Emit for default formatter to bypass Environment::format call and branch directly to write_escaped
Tested Value handle registry switch from BTreeMap to HashMap for Value::serialize roundtrip path
Tried larger initial VM stack capacity (32) to reduce Value Vec reallocations
Checked existing unit assertion confirming Value size is 24 bytes on 64-bit targets
Tried unsafe unchecked VM stack pop to reduce Option/unwrap overhead on Value moves
Refactored internal Value roundtrip handle registry from keyed map to stack-based pop path in serializer
Switched VM frame locals from BTreeMap to HashMap to test faster local lookup/store
Hoisted default formatter and strict undefined checks out of VM loop for Emit instruction
Tried manual ASCII lowercase byte scan in upper filter no-op fast path
Repeat measurement for manual ASCII lowercase scan in upper fast path
Added Object::get_value_by_str path and specialized map lookups to avoid temporary Value key allocations (run 1)
Add Object::get_value_by_str fast path and optimize Value-keyed map string lookups for small maps
Tried manual loop implementation for small Value-map string lookups instead of iterator find_map
Tuned Value-map get_value_by_str linear-scan threshold from <=8 to <=16
Tuned Value-map get_value_by_str linear-scan threshold from <=8 to <=4
Control run on current best code to observe machine noise after Value lookup optimization
Forced inline on Value::get_attr and get_attr_fast after get_value_by_str refactor
Added get_value_by_str overrides for MergeDict and Namespace objects to avoid temporary Value keys
Tried splitting Option arg-count handling at VM call sites to favor fixed-arity paths
Use string-keyed BTreeMap in hidden context! builder to avoid Value-key conversion overhead
Follow-up measurement of string-keyed context map showed significant run-to-run variance under current load
Tried static-str context map keys with direct Object impl for BTreeMap<&'static str, _> to eliminate key allocations
Prototype context! ContextMap object failed to compile due Debug impl using wrong iterator item type
Tested custom Vec-backed ContextMap object for context! macro to avoid tree map overhead
Match ValueRepr::String/SmallStr directly in small Value-map string lookup fast path
Tried manual loop instead of find_map closure for small Value-map string-key lookup
Tried thread-local Arc<str> key interning in context! builder to avoid repeated Arc allocations
Add Loop::get_value_by_str fast path for loop attribute access to skip temporary Value key creation
Tried VM-level special case for loop.index in GetAttr to bypass generic object attribute lookup
Tried restructuring Loop::get_value_by_str to defer len computation to specific match arms
Forced inline on Loop get_value/get_value_by_str after loop attribute fast path
Forced inline(always) for Output write methods and fmt::Write impl methods
Control run on current best commit to monitor benchmark variance after recent experiments
Tried early-return fast path for loop.index before full Loop attribute match
Cached loop object Value in LoopState to avoid rebuilding Value::from_dyn_object on loop lookups
Retried ASCII to_ascii_uppercase path for upper filter after recent VM/object optimizations
Prototype Vec-backed Locals failed due visibility and iterator type mismatches across vm modules
Use Vec-backed Locals with linear lookup/update instead of BTreeMap for small VM scope frames
Tried manual for-loop implementations for Locals insert/get instead of iterator find/find_map
Preallocate Vec-backed Locals with capacity 4 to reduce scope-local insertion reallocations
Tune initial Locals vector capacity from 4 to 8 for lower reallocation overhead
Run interrupted by late-night continuation guard before benchmark execution
Further tune initial Locals vector capacity from 8 to 16
Tune initial Locals vector capacity from 16 to 32 for lower allocation churn in deep loop scopes
Tried increasing Locals initial vector capacity to 64
Expanded harness to also emit parse/compile secondary metrics; render metric slightly higher on first run
Expand autoresearch harness with parse/compile secondary metrics while preserving render as primary
Tried Locals initial capacity 24 instead of 32 under expanded multi-metric harness
Tried Locals initial capacity 40 instead of 32
Tried reverse-iteration strategy for Locals get/insert to favor recently touched variables
Added aggressive inline(always) hints to Vec-backed Locals helper methods
Retested larger VM evaluation stack capacity (32) with current optimized locals design
Replaced Value handle BTreeMap with Vec<Option<Value>> indexed storage for serde roundtrip path
Tried last-entry fast path in Locals::insert for repeated writes to same variable
Added first-entry shortcut in Locals::get before linear scan
Tried pointer-equality fast path for Vec-backed Locals key matching before string equality
Tried Locals last-lookup index cache via Cell to speed repeated variable access
Added has_loop_local flag to skip locals scan on loop lookups unless shadowed
Tune Value-keyed map string-lookup linear-scan threshold from <=8 to <=12
Tried raising Value-map string-lookup linear-scan threshold from <=12 to <=16
Tried lowering Value-map string-lookup linear-scan threshold from <=12 to <=10
Attempted force-inline on Context::load hot variable lookup path
Tried LoopState local index + atomic store instead of atomic fetch_add in loop iteration
Control run on unchanged code showed lower render time; treated as measurement variance
Tried Vec-accumulate then collect-to-BTreeMap strategy in context! builder instead of direct BTreeMap inserts
Initial run for VM stack capacity 24 showed strong improvement; running confirmation
Tune VM evaluation stack initial capacity from 16 to 24
Tried VM stack initial capacity 20 (vs 24)
Tried VM stack initial capacity 28 (vs 24)
Tried VM stack initial capacity 22 (vs 24)
Tried VM stack initial capacity 26 (vs 24)
Retested Locals initial capacity 40 under new stack-capacity-24 baseline
Tried Locals initial capacity 36 (first run showed marginal improvement, awaiting confirmation)
Confirmation run for Locals capacity 36 regressed; keeping capacity 32
Tried reducing Context frame-stack initial capacity from 32 to 16
Tried reducing Context frame-stack initial capacity from 32 to 24
Initial run for Context frame-stack capacity 40 showed improvement; running confirmation
Tune Context frame-stack initial capacity from 32 to 40
Tried increasing Context frame-stack initial capacity from 40 to 48
Tried reducing Context frame-stack initial capacity from 40 to 36
Tried Context frame-stack initial capacity 44
Tried VM value stack initial capacity 23 under context-stack-40 baseline
Tried VM value stack initial capacity 25 under context-stack-40 baseline
Prototype sorted Vec-backed ContextMap with binary-search lookups for context! macro values
Preallocated auto_escape_stack with capacity 8 in VM eval loop
Retested Locals value-vector initial capacity 40 with current context-stack-40 baseline
Tried Locals value-vector initial capacity 28 with current baseline
Tried Context frame-stack initial capacity 42
Tried Context frame-stack initial capacity 38
Control run on current best commit to monitor variance after capacity tuning sweep
Split Context::load into dedicated loop-key and non-loop-key paths to remove per-frame key=="loop" check
Tried reverse-order scan only in Locals::insert to favor recent variable rewrites
Tried reverse-order scan in Locals::get to favor most recently inserted locals
Forced inline(always) on Stack::get_call_args
Tried macro-level fast path to bypass Value::from_serialize for nested context! expressions
Tried split-key/value Vec representation for Locals to reduce value loads during key scans
Tried early-return split in Loop::get_value_by_str to skip len handling for common fields
Tried manual for-loop implementation for Locals::insert update path
Tried Locals initial capacity 34 between previous 32 and 36 values
Tried Context frame-stack initial capacity 39
Tried hoisting default formatter flag out of VM instruction loop
Tried Context frame-stack initial capacity 41
Control run on current best commit after recent unsuccessful tuning attempts
Control run before new changes; observed higher-noise sample on current machine load
Tried caching Loop length as precomputed u64 to avoid repeated usize->u64 casts
Retried internal Value handle registry using HashMap instead of BTreeMap under current best baseline
Prototyped inline-small Locals storage (8 inline slots + overflow vec) to reduce heap traffic
Tried Vec<(handle, Value)> registry for internal Value serialization roundtrip instead of BTreeMap
Tried manual for-loop implementation for Locals::get instead of iterator find_map
Tried tracking did_iterate flag in LoopState to avoid atomic load in did_not_iterate
Retested Locals initial capacity 24 under current best (stack-24, frame-stack-40) baseline
Tried increasing Value-map string lookup linear-scan threshold from <=12 to <=14
Tried lowering Value-map string lookup linear-scan threshold from <=12 to <=11
Baseline: Value size is 24 bytes on 64-bit target (value::tests::test_sizes)
Prototype 16-byte Value repr (Arc-backed i128/u128/string/object + smaller SmallStr) failed to compile due moved-from borrowed matches; needs borrow-pattern rewrites

name: Recover render performance after Value size reduction

description: Add Seq ValueRepr variant for serialized/iter-collected lists to avoid Arc<DynObject> wrapper overhead in render hot path
Add StrMap ValueRepr variant for context! maps and direct map attr/item paths to reduce Arc<DynObject> overhead while keeping 16-byte Value
Cache LoopState object Value and reuse StrMap/Seq variants to avoid repeated Arc<DynObject> allocations; recovers most render regression while keeping 16-byte Value
Fix StrMap semantic regression with minimal overhead: make dict() emit StrMap only when cloning an existing map with string keys, preserving kwargs behavior and snapshot order
Restore compatibility for Seq/StrMap in ArgType and mixed-map equality/ordering while retaining performance; tests pass and render slightly improves
Post-compaction control run on current StrMap/Seq-compatible 16-byte Value branch; observed temporary drift upward (~32.1us) likely machine noise
Speed up VM locals lookup/update by replacing iterator-closure scans with manual loops and pointer-equality fast checks for interned variable names
Confirmation run after locals pointer-equality patch (no code changes); measured ~30.2us, indicating positive noise but experiment already captured
Tried thread-local Arc<str> interning for context! keys to avoid per-render key allocations; hash/refcell overhead regressed render throughput
Tried precomputing key=="loop" once in Context::load to skip repeated loop-var checks; no gain and regressed
Tried manual for-loop implementation for Context::current_loop to avoid iterator adapter overhead; render regressed significantly
Add primitive fast paths in ArgType<Cow<str>> (u64/i64/bool) to avoid generic Value Display conversion overhead on hot filter calls (notably item|upper)
Control rerun on same primitive string-conversion fast path showed substantial noise drift upward (~30.7us); keep prior improved code but treat single-run variance cautiously
Tried small static cache for common integer->string conversions in ArgType<Cow<str>> plus bool static borrow; slightly worse than prior primitive fast-path approach
Tried callable cache in VM with BoxedFunction fast-path dispatch for filters/tests to bypass Value::call; extra classification/indirection regressed heavily
Added specialized builtin upper_filter(Value)->Value and registered it as default upper, bypassing generic Cow<String> arg marshalling and using allocation-free integer/boolean uppercase paths
Fixed strict/semi-strict undefined semantics in specialized upper_filter by adding state-based undefined checks; correctness restored but performance regressed notably
Retried upper specialization with semantics-preserving Cow->Value wrapper (upper_to_value) to cut no-op string allocations; still slower than ArgType primitive fast-path baseline

Reverted specialized upper_filter fast path from previous run because it violated strict/semi-strict undefined semantics; restored correct built-in upper behavior while retaining major ArgType speedups
Tried single-pass ASCII/lowercase detection in upper filter to replace is_ascii()+any double scan; regressed on workload
Tried bool-to-string static borrow in ArgType<Cow<str>> to avoid tiny allocation; no benefit on this workload
Reintroduced specialized upper_filter with strict-undefined correctness via custom CheckedValue ArgType, plus allocation-free u64/i64/bool uppercase paths
Control rerun on CheckedValue-based upper_filter to gauge variance; still around 27us and confirms large improvement over prior valid baseline
Optimize upper_filter hot path by deferring kwargs-type check to Object branch only, avoiding per-call is_kwargs overhead for primitive/string inputs
Further optimize upper_filter by switching CheckedValue wrapper to direct (&State, &Value) args: avoid Value cloning while preserving strict undefined checks and object-only kwargs guard
Control rerun on (&State,&Value)-based upper_filter optimization confirms stable ~25us performance band
Bypass generic Value::from_function argument marshalling for upper by registering a dedicated UpperFilterObject with direct call(args) dispatch and optimized coercion path
Control rerun on dedicated UpperFilterObject path; confirms another very strong sample (~24.2us) with current implementation
Add VM ApplyFilter fast path for built-in upper: when cached filter is UpperFilterObject, bypass generic Value::call and dispatch directly to call_upper_filter_fast
Tried caching UpperFilterObject downcast classification per local_id in VM ApplyFilter to avoid repeated checks; added branching/state overhead regressed
Tried extra VM upper fast path specialization for single-arg calls (call_upper_filter_one) to skip args slice matching; added branches regressed
Add cached small-u64 Value table for upper filter coercion (0..1023) and reuse for non-negative i64 path, avoiding repeated decimal conversion in hot numeric upper calls
Control rerun after cached small-u64 upper coercion; result remains in ~23.5us range and confirms large win
Tried arg-count-aware one-arg upper VM dispatch (call_upper_filter_one) to skip slice arity match on fixed-arity instructions; no improvement
Tune upper numeric Value cache size from 1024 to 256 entries (covers common small integers with better locality and less initialization overhead)
Control rerun for 256-entry upper numeric cache confirms stable performance around 23.35us
Tried shrinking upper numeric cache to 128 entries; misses for common mid-range integers outweighed locality gains
Tried expanding upper numeric cache to 512 entries; no measurable win versus 256-entry cache
Tried simplifying cached_u64_value to return Value directly (removing Option/unwrap_or path); no measurable gain
Add small-integer HTML output fast path in write_with_html_escaping for U64/I64/Bool using cached string formatting, bypassing generic Value display on hot numeric emits
Control rerun on numeric HTML output fast path confirms stable ~21.7us band
Tried shrinking small-integer HTML output cache from 256 to 128; misses on mid-range loop indices regressed render
Tried expanding small-integer HTML output cache from 256 to 512; no measurable improvement
Baseline run on multi-workload autoresearch harness (all_elements + string_heavy + macro_heavy) after reinitializing target
Optimize context! construction by storing static keys as &'static str maps and adding direct Object map support, avoiding per-key Arc<str> allocations across all render workloads
Control rerun on unchanged static-key context optimization measured lower render_ns (~73.26us); treated as favorable variance until confirmed by additional code-backed gains
Attempted small-map linear-scan fast path for string-keyed map get_value_by_str; failed to compile due ambiguous AsRef type inference in generic map-key macro
Tested small-map linear-scan path for string-keyed BTreeMap attribute lookup (len<=8) to avoid tree lookup overhead; regressed multi-workload render time
Special-case nested context! values in __context_pair to bypass Value::from_serialize roundtrip for context! subexpressions, reducing nested context conversion overhead
Control rerun on nested-context serialization bypass showed higher sample (~74.8us), indicating notable run-to-run noise; keep prior improvement but continue validating
Optimize Value serialization handle registry with a single-slot fast path plus BTreeMap overflow to reduce insert/remove overhead for common immediate Value roundtrips in context construction
Control rerun after single-slot Value handle registry optimization stayed in the same ~73.1us band, confirming improvement but no further gain without code changes
Tried BasicStore single-template fast path in Environment::get to bypass map lookup when only one template is loaded; primary render gain was tiny/noisy and not robust
Preallocated Output capture stack with capacity 4 to reduce capture push reallocations; observed only tiny/noisy primary change on multi-workload benchmark
Replace closure-based internal-serialization OnDrop guard with lightweight dedicated Drop guard to reduce Value::from_serialize guard overhead on hot context construction paths
Remove now-unused OnDrop utility after switching to dedicated serialization guard, reducing dead code and preserving optimized guard path
Control rerun on current optimized branch remained in ~71.3-71.7us range, confirming materially improved performance but no additional gain without code changes

Streamline Value roundtrip signaling by switching handle transport from tuple-struct marker to direct newtype-struct handling in ValueSerializer, removing per-field tuple machinery on hot from_serialize paths
Control rerun on newtype-based Value handle signaling remained in ~71.1-71.3us range, confirming stable improvement without additional code changes
Tried single-slot handle id shortcut (fixed handle 0 fast path) to avoid LAST_VALUE_HANDLE increments for common Value roundtrips; unexpectedly regressed render throughput
Add context! literal fast path in __context_pair to use Value::from directly for literals, bypassing serde-based from_serialize conversion overhead on common literal fields
Control rerun on literal fast path landed in the same improved ~70.8-71.0us band, indicating stable gains with normal measurement variance
Tried context! vector-of-context! specialization to build Value sequences directly from nested context! entries; added macro complexity and regressed overall render throughput
Tried Context::load fast path for frames with empty locals/no loop state to skip map/loop checks; additional branch and is_empty checks regressed multi-workload render
Retested a narrower context! vec-of-context! specialization (brace form only); result was effectively within noise relative to current best and not a robust improvement
Control run on current kept optimizations (no code changes) to re-establish variance band; render remained around 70.8us
Tried one-entry thread-local Arc<str> cache for non-small Value::from(&str) conversions to reuse repeated literal allocations; cache lookup overhead outweighed savings and regressed render
Resume-session control run on current optimized baseline to re-establish variance before new experiments
Tried increasing compile-time buffer_size_hint multiplier from 2x to 3x template raw bytes to reduce render-time String growth reallocations; initial sample improved slightly, pending confirmation
Confirmation run for buffer_size_hint 3x multiplier regressed versus current baseline; treating prior improvement as noise and reverting this tuning
Tried context! fast path for vec![literal, ...] to build Value sequences directly and bypass serde conversion for literal lists; regressed total render time
Tried dedicated HandleSerializer for VALUE_HANDLE_MARKER newtype path to avoid transform(value).as_usize handle extraction; added serializer machinery regressed render throughput
Tried avoiding redundant INTERNAL_SERIALIZATION set(true) when already true in mark_internal_serialization; unexpectedly regressed render throughput
Tried String->Value conversion via Arc::from(val.into_boxed_str()) for large strings to reuse/move string storage path; regressed render workloads
Tried forcing inline(always) on Value::from_serialize to reduce call overhead across context construction; no improvement on multi-workload render benchmark
Refactored context! macro into split no-merge and merge-specific arms to remove empty merge-array branch in common path; unexpectedly caused major render regression
Tried forcing inline(always) on serializing_for_value thread-local check; did not improve and measured substantial regression
Tried reducing buffer_size_hint to 1x raw template bytes (next power of two) to cut initial String allocation size; render regressed versus current 2x heuristic
Tried BasicStore small-map (<=2 templates) linear-scan lookup in Environment::get to reduce BTreeMap lookup overhead for tiny template sets; regressed render
Added positional-only fast path in Macro::call to skip kwargs BTreeSet bookkeeping when no kwargs are present; minor macro_heavy gain was outweighed by regressions elsewhere
Tried replacing Value handle registry overflow storage from BTreeMap to small Vec<(u32, Value)> with linear remove/swap_remove for tiny expected cardinality; slightly regressed render
Add ASCII fast path for title filter using byte-wise case conversion and preallocated output, avoiding per-char Unicode formatter writes on common ASCII text
Control rerun after title ASCII fast path stayed in stronger ~69.7-70.1us band, confirming robust improvement across string-heavy and macro-heavy workloads
Tried adding ASCII fast path to lower filter (no-op detection + to_ascii_lowercase); regressed total render due all_elements slowdown despite minor string-heavy gains
Tried changing default filter's optional fallback argument from Option<Value> to Option<&Value> to avoid eager Value cloning in argument marshalling; regressed total render
Added replace filter fast paths for no-op cases (from==to and substring-miss) to avoid unnecessary String replacement work; initial run improved but requires confirmation against variance
Optimize replace filter with no-op fast paths (identical from/to and missing substring) to skip unnecessary allocation-heavy replacement work across string-heavy and macro-heavy templates
Tried single-byte ASCII replace specialization using memchr + in-place byte rewrite for from.len()==to.len()==1; added overhead and regressed render throughput
Tried byte-vector ASCII path for title filter (Vec<u8> + from_utf8_unchecked) to avoid per-char String::push overhead; did not beat existing title fast path
Tried replace precheck tuning with memchr for single-byte patterns instead of generic contains; did not outperform current replace fast-path implementation
Control run on current kept optimizations to re-check variance; render remains in ~69.6-70.0us band
Tried using to_ascii_uppercase for ASCII uppercase conversions when lowercase bytes are present; small improvement signal but within noise band, needs confirmation
Confirmation run for ASCII to_ascii_uppercase path regressed notably (especially macro-heavy), so keeping existing upper implementation
Retuned Context frame-stack initial capacity from 40 to 36 under current multi-workload baseline; regressed render performance
Retuned Context frame-stack initial capacity from 40 to 44; initial run showed strong improvement, pending confirmation
Confirmation run for Context stack capacity 44 regressed back toward/no better than baseline; prior strong sample treated as variance
Retuned Context frame-stack initial capacity from 40 to 42; initial run showed strong improvements across all three render workloads, pending confirmation
Confirmation run for Context stack capacity 42 regressed into existing variance band; not a robust improvement over current baseline
Control run on current kept code after capacity retune attempts; observed lower-noise sample (~69.56us) without code changes
Tried context! vec[...] specialization to build Value sequences directly from per-item from_serialize conversions; result was effectively flat/slightly worse vs current best
Tried replacing Value roundtrip handle registry with simple LIFO stack + unit-struct marker (removing handle generation/map lookup); caused clear multi-workload regression
Tried join filter micro-optimizations (reserve joiner capacity from val.len() and first-flag loop instead of enumerate); effectively flat/slightly worse than current best
Tune replace fast path to only run substring-miss precheck for multi-byte patterns; avoids redundant double scans on common single-character replacements while still skipping obvious no-op cases
Control rerun after replace precheck tuning produced a high-noise outlier (~71.5us); continuing with further validation of new keep
Second control rerun after replace tuning also showed severe machine-load drift; sample treated as outlier and not used to judge code quality
Tried tightening replace miss-precheck threshold from pattern length >1 to >2; regressed relative to current replace tuning
Tried branch pattern-match delimiter check in title ASCII fast path instead of is_ascii_punctuation/is_ascii_whitespace helpers; regressed render throughput
Restored tuple-struct Value roundtrip marker semantics to fix flattening error behavior/tests; primary benchmark regressed heavily under current run conditions

Cached default auto-escape callbacks and formatter Arcs in Environment::new/empty with OnceLock so each environment clone reuses shared callbacks instead of allocating new Arc trait objects; compile improved to 10293.95ns (parse 6842.12ns) with stable render guardrail 81387.37ns

Added ASCII fast path in Tokenizer::skip_whitespace with Unicode fallback. Run was heavily contended and produced unusable outlier metrics (parse 10565.10ns, compile 13512.05ns, render 126552.08ns), so discard pending cleaner measurements
Second post-change control run remained severely saturated (parse 17512.04ns, compile 22656.25ns, render 208615.89ns), confirming host contention is dominating measurements

Tried reverting parse_args preallocation to Vec::new to reduce empty-call allocations. Host was extremely contended (run 35.7s; parse 25045.09ns, compile 27530.93ns, render 228207.69ns), so sample is unusable and discarded

Control run immediately after killing a long-lived rogue autoresearch_render background process; metrics improved from extreme saturation but remained elevated (parse 8239.75ns, compile 10691.73ns, render 168709.31ns), so treated as transitional noise
Follow-up control remained highly unstable even without rogue benchmark processes (parse 14136.72ns, compile 18325.84ns, render 192068.36ns), so still unusable as optimization signal

Cached last recorded line/span in Instructions to avoid Vec::last checks in add_with_line/add_with_span. This run showed a huge improvement (parse 5861.82ns, compile 7571.62ns, render 68945.65ns), but coincided with a major host-load shift; discarding until A/B confirmed

Second run of cached-last-line/span Instructions change remained in the same strong band (parse 6030.76ns, compile 7765.62ns, render 71068.36ns), suggesting improvement may be real; still pending baseline A/B under same load

A/B baseline rerun on unmodified Instructions (after reverting cached-last-line/span patch) measured parse 5906.25ns and compile 7594.08ns, essentially matching or slightly beating patched runs; treating the patch as non-beneficial
Retuned codegen instruction buffer preallocation from 256 to 224 entries; compile regressed slightly to 7673.83ns (parse 5913.57ns), so 256 remains better

Changed parser call-argument vector initialization from Vec::with_capacity(4) to Vec::new to avoid eager allocations for zero-arg calls; first run improved to parse 5807.62ns and compile 7386.40ns, pending confirmation
Confirmation run for parse_args Vec::new remained better on parse (5849.61ns) but compile returned to baseline (7593.10ns); net gain shrank to a small/noisy ~0.4%, so not yet keep-worthy

Use Vec::new for parse_args instead of eager Vec::with_capacity(4), avoiding unnecessary allocations for common zero-arg calls; repeated runs stabilized around parse ~5.82µs with compile ~7.60µs (this run 5818.85ns + 7597.98ns) and no render regression
Retuned parser subparse statement vector capacity from 16 to 8 to reduce over-allocation in many small nested blocks; first run improved to parse 5884.77ns and compile 7441.08ns, pending confirmation
Second run for subparse capacity 8 was a severe contention outlier (parse 17553.39ns, compile 18466.15ns, render 168690.44ns); cannot use for decision
Third run for subparse capacity 8 under normal load regressed (parse 5928.06ns, compile 7770.51ns), so keeping subparse capacity 16
Retuned parse_with_block assignment vector capacity from 4 to 2 to better match typical small assignment counts; first run improved parse to 5757.16ns and compile to 7441.09ns, pending confirmation
Second run for parse_with_block capacity 2 remained in improved band (parse 5732.59ns, compile 7502.28ns, render 69215.50ns), running one more validation before keeping

Reduce parse_with_block assignment Vec preallocation from 4 to 2, matching common small assignment counts and cutting parser allocation overhead; validated across multiple runs with parse ~5.74-5.76µs and compile ~7.44-7.50µs (this run 5762.86ns + 7489.59ns) without render regression
Retuned parse_assignment tuple target buffer from capacity 2 to 1; this increased parse/compile to 5866.54ns + 7563.48ns, so capacity 2 remains better
Retuned parser subparse statement Vec capacity from 16 to 12; compile improved slightly (7396.48ns) but parse regressed (5890.95ns), net parse+compile was effectively flat/slightly worse
Further retuned parse_with_block assignment capacity from 2 to 1; parse improved slightly but compile worsened (7471.68ns), leaving total parse+compile effectively unchanged/slightly worse
Added Tokenizer::advance fast paths for 1-byte advances and ASCII no-newline slices to avoid per-char loops; this regressed parse/compile to 5949.87ns + 7740.23ns, so reverted

Refactored TokenStream to store lookahead token and pending error in separate fields (Option<Token> + Option<Error>) instead of Option<Result<...>> to reduce hot-path matching overhead; first run was roughly flat/slightly worse overall (parse 5904.62ns, compile 7412.43ns) pending confirmation
Confirmation run for TokenStream pending-error refactor regressed clearly (parse 6077.31ns, compile 7650.71ns, render 71991.87ns), so reverting
Re-tested codegen filter/test local-id cache as small Vec linear lookup instead of BTreeMap; regressed parse/compile (6095.70ns + 7597.66ns), so keep BTreeMap approach

Interned default syntax delimiters behind OnceLock (reuse Arc<Delims> in SyntaxConfig::default and builder) to avoid per-default Arc allocation; first run was essentially flat/slightly worse (parse 5721.68ns, compile 7536.46ns) pending confirmation
Second run for default-delimiter Arc interning improved to parse 5730.30ns and compile 7390.62ns (parse+compile 13120.92ns); signal looks promising but needs another run to separate from variance

Intern default SyntaxConfig delimiters with OnceLock and reuse Arc<Delims> in SyntaxConfig::default/builder instead of allocating new Arcs each time; validated over multiple runs with parse ~5.69-5.73µs and compile ~7.35-7.39µs (this run 5693.04ns + 7350.59ns) and stable render guardrail

Tried branch-ordered default_auto_escape_callback rewrite (explicit suffix checks + rsplit_once) to reduce string-matching overhead on template registration; regressed to parse 5846.84ns and compile 7520.51ns with higher render guardrail
Tried using str::find('{') in lexer start-marker scan instead of byte memchr helper; parse/compile regressed (5989.91ns + 7625.98ns) and left utils::memchr unused, so reverting
Re-tested caching default/empty TemplateConfig behind OnceLock in Environment::new/empty; regressed parse+compile to 5847.01ns + 7617.19ns, so per-call TemplateConfig::new remains faster
Added conditional gate to avoid calling should_lstrip_block when lstrip logic cannot apply; this increased compile time (7723.30ns) and regressed total parse+compile
Split parser subparse capacities (top-level 16, nested 8) to reduce over-allocation in nested bodies while keeping root capacity; net parse+compile regressed slightly (5796.88ns + 7551.43ns)

Reduced initial line/span info vector capacities in Instructions::new from 128 to 64 to cut compile-time allocation overhead; first run improved to parse 5670.09ns and compile 7302.73ns, pending confirmation
Second run for line/span capacity 64 regressed markedly (parse 5872.72ns, compile 7447.91ns), so initial improvement signal appears unstable
Third run for line/span capacity 64 remained worse (parse 5782.55ns, compile 7640.30ns); keeping existing 128 capacities
Retuned instruction buffer preallocation from 256 to 272 entries; first run was nearly flat/slightly worse (parse 5707.19ns, compile 7362.95ns), pending quick validation
Second run for instruction capacity 272 improved to parse 5736.16ns and compile 7232.74ns (12968.90ns total); running one more validation to determine if this is robust
Third validation for instruction capacity 272 regressed (parse 5797.04ns, compile 7409.83ns), so keeping instruction capacity at 256
Control run on current kept code to re-establish variance band: parse 5727.54ns, compile 7483.07ns, render 68522.46ns
Cached current active span in CodeGenerator to avoid span_stack.last() lookup in add/push/pop paths; this regressed parse+compile slightly (5897.46ns + 7390.62ns)
Re-tested parser block-duplicate tracking with small Vec instead of BTreeSet; first run was nearly flat (parse 5660.81ns improved, compile 7398.44ns worsened) and needs confirmation
Confirmation run for Vec-based block tracking regressed to parse 5760.09ns and compile 7432.95ns; keep existing BTreeSet implementation
Changed parser::parse signature to take &SyntaxConfig, but first benchmark build failed because autoresearch harness still passed SyntaxConfig by value
After fixing call sites for parse(&SyntaxConfig), avoiding one syntax_config clone in CompiledTemplate path regressed to parse 5791.83ns and compile 7498.70ns; no benefit

Re-applied non-loader BasicStore single-template fast path for fresh A/B under current conditions. Compile improved strongly to 7253.91ns (parse 5848.14ns), but render guardrail increased to 71362.63ns; validating whether render hit is consistent

Second validation of BasicStore single-template path stayed strongly improved on primary (parse 5779.62ns, compile 7179.36ns) but render guardrail remained elevated at 70782.56ns (~3%+ above control band)

Third validation for BasicStore single-template fast path kept render elevated (71163.08ns) while primary gains fluctuated (5898.77ns parse, 7371.09ns compile). Discarding due consistent render regression risk

Added direct default-syntax start-marker path in tokenize_root to bypass generic find_start_marker dispatch; result was effectively flat/slightly worse (5793.78ns parse, 7420.90ns compile) and introduced an unused-function warning

Added Instructions::new_with_capacities and switched codegen subgenerators (for blocks/macros) to smaller initial buffers (64 instructions / 32 line+span infos, smaller pending_block vec) to cut compile-time over-allocation; first run improved to parse 5699.71ns and compile 7127.93ns, pending confirmation
Second run for smaller subgenerator capacities landed essentially flat vs current baseline (parse 5824.87ns, compile 7219.73ns); collecting one more sample
Third validation for reduced subgenerator capacities regressed (parse 5909.50ns, compile 7401.37ns), so no robust win; reverting this approach

Reduced CodeGenerator pending_block/span_stack prealloc capacities from 32 to 16 to cut compile-time allocation overhead; first run improved to parse 5657.23ns and compile 7280.93ns, pending confirmation
Second run for CodeGenerator capacity 16 remained slightly improved (parse 5741.54ns, compile 7290.04ns) with render guardrail stable; collecting third sample
Third validation for CodeGenerator capacities 16/16 regressed to parse 5798.34ns and compile 7477.86ns, so the lower-capacity signal was not robust
Replaced RESERVED_NAMES array contains() check with direct matches! in parse_assign_name; this regressed parse/compile (5809.73ns + 7615.88ns), so reverted

Replaced lexer start-marker search with a single-pass manual byte scan (removing helper memchr call) and observed a modest first-run improvement (parse 5845.86ns, compile 7326.82ns), but introduced dead-code warning for now-unused utils::memchr; pending confirmation
Second validation for manual lexer byte-scan was effectively flat (parse 5693.03ns, compile 7514.32ns) and still triggered unused memchr warning; not a robust win
Control run on current kept code: parse 5704.43ns, compile 7396.16ns, render 68084.62ns (healthy guardrail)
Retuned CodeGenerator pending_block/span_stack prealloc from 32 to 24; first run improved to parse 5744.30ns and compile 7256.18ns with render guardrail stable
Second run for CodeGenerator capacity 24 was worse (parse 5703.94ns, compile 7440.10ns), indicating unstable signal; collecting final sample
Third validation for CodeGenerator capacities 24/24 came back near baseline (parse 5750.98ns, compile 7338.55ns); no robust improvement over existing settings
Specialized utils::memstr for 1-byte and 2-byte needles (used by lexer comment/raw searches); regressed parse/compile to 5801.60ns + 7469.40ns, so reverted

Preallocated lexer state stack with capacity 8 in Tokenizer::new (instead of vec![..] capacity 1) to avoid stack Vec growth during nested template/block transitions; first run improved parse to 5531.57ns and compile to 7366.54ns, pending confirmation
Second run for lexer stack preallocation strengthened improvement signal: parse 5423.34ns, compile 7205.73ns, render 67656.26ns; running third validation

Pre-size Tokenizer state stack to capacity 8 at construction (instead of single-element vec![]) to avoid stack Vec growth in nested block/variable transitions; validated across runs with parse 5.42-5.65µs and compile 7.20-7.37µs (this run 5647.14ns + 7297.85ns) and improved render guardrail
Retuned Tokenizer stack preallocation from 8 to 4; first run was slightly worse overall (parse 5691.08ns, compile 7277.67ns) but near noise band
Second run with Tokenizer stack capacity 4 remained slightly slower than capacity 8 baseline (parse 5741.05ns, compile 7225.26ns); keeping capacity 8

Tightened find_start_marker_memchr loop to compute absolute index once and use direct byte indexing for marker detection; first run improved to parse 5655.76ns and compile 7270.51ns, pending confirmation
Second run for tighter find_start_marker_memchr regressed toward baseline (parse 5749.19ns, compile 7303.71ns); collecting final sample
Third validation for tightened find_start_marker_memchr remained effectively flat/slightly worse than current best (parse 5542.97ns, compile 7415.70ns); not keeping
Control run after reverting lexer-marker loop tweaks: parse 5662.43ns, compile 7422.20ns, render 68051.43ns

Collapsed builtin map initialization into one cached tuple (`get_all_builtins`) and updated Environment::new to fetch all registries via a single OnceLock path; result was effectively flat/slightly worse (parse 5679.69ns, compile 7396.81ns)
Control run on current kept code under lower host noise: parse 5624.02ns, compile 7199.22ns, render 67926.76ns
Retuned Tokenizer stack preallocation from 8 to 12; this clearly regressed parse/compile (5791.02ns + 7684.57ns), so keeping capacity 8
Tried byte-based trailing-newline trim in Tokenizer::new to avoid multiple ends_with calls; regressed parse/compile to 5660.48ns + 7405.92ns
Tried parser block-name tracking with HashSet instead of BTreeSet; parse regressed to 5873.05ns and total parse+compile worsened (compile 7357.74ns)
Retuned Tokenizer stack capacity from 8 to 6; first run landed between prior samples (parse 5595.05ns, compile 7298.83ns), requiring confirmation
Second run with Tokenizer stack capacity 6 remained in similar band (parse 5654.62ns, compile 7229.82ns); performing direct A/B against current capacity 8 baseline
A/B baseline with current capacity-8 lexer stack measured parse 5429.52ns and compile 7294.59ns, clearly outperforming capacity-6 trials; keep capacity 8
Retuned parse_with_block assignment capacity from 2 to 3; this regressed parse/compile materially (5820.48ns + 7407.88ns)
Retuned instruction buffer capacity from 256 to 240; parse/compile regressed to 5768.07ns + 7490.88ns
Replaced iterator-based block/variable whitespace skip with manual byte loop in lexer tokenization; this regressed significantly (parse 6215.01ns, compile 7872.07ns)
Forced inline(always) on find_start_marker_memchr in lexer; regressed to parse 5851.73ns and compile 7362.30ns

Added adaptive subgenerator preallocation using block body size hints (custom Instructions::new_with_capacities + tuned pending-block capacity) to reduce block compile allocation overhead; first run showed a modest improvement to parse 5743.49ns, compile 7291.99ns pending confirmation
Second run for adaptive subgenerator capacities regressed (parse 5872.72ns, compile 7433.27ns), so signal is not robust and change will be reverted
Added compile_call_args fast path for empty arg lists with no caller to bypass setup/scans; run regressed (parse 5794.11ns, compile 7552.09ns) and showed elevated render guardrail
Second run of empty-args compile_call_args fast path remained worse (parse 5848.63ns, compile 7478.84ns); reverting
Control run on current kept code during transient load increase (parse 5987.14ns, compile 7489.26ns, render 70603.84ns); treated as noise

Tried root-level parser child-vector capacity heuristic based on remaining source length (subparse_with_capacity + tokenizer remaining_len); this regressed parse/compile (5901.21ns + 7555.98ns) and raised render guardrail
Replaced utils::memchr iterator-position closure with manual enumerated loop; regressed parse/compile to 5782.23ns + 7478.19ns
Tried changing parser subparse callback from &dyn Fn to generic impl Fn for monomorphization, but ran into higher-ranked lifetime/closure signature mismatches across call sites
Removed dead first_span bookkeeping in parse_args keyword-argument handling; this regressed parse/compile to 5778.64ns + 7530.93ns
Prototyped inline lexer state stack with overflow fallback to avoid heap-backed Vec for context state; added branching/regressed significantly (parse 6171.55ns, compile 7707.35ns)
Replaced dynamic subparse end-check closures with enum-driven EndCheck matching to remove trait-object dispatch; regressed parse/compile (5888.67ns + 7486.00ns) and raised render guardrail

Special-cased tokenize_root marker lookup for non-custom syntax to call find_start_marker_memchr(self.rest()) directly (removing generic non-custom wrapper); regressed parse/compile and render guardrail

Serialize serde structs as BTreeMap<&'static str, Value> object maps (context!-style) instead of Value-keyed ValueMap to avoid per-field Value key construction and accelerate string field lookups; comparison render dropped to 4024.09ns and total to 8301.19ns
Validation run on struct-map serialization change confirmed strong sustained gain (comparison ~8.31µs) but not below prior best sample; guardrail render settled at 68493.82ns
Attempted small-map linear-scan fast path for string-key map get_value_by_str; macro-generic AsRef comparison caused type inference errors across key types and failed to compile

Scoped small-map linear-scan fast path to only &'static str keyed maps (keeping direct get for String/Arc<str> maps); this improved guardrail render pressure but was still slightly slower than best comparison sample

Replace serde struct serialization backing store with sorted Vec<(&'static str, Value)> wrapped in a lightweight StaticKeyMap object to avoid BTreeMap node allocations while preserving deterministic key order; comparison render improved to 3922.85ns
Mark serialization error conversion as cold (to_invalid_value) and keep transform hot path focused on successful ValueSerializer output; comparison render dropped to 3785.16ns and total to 7986.33ns
Added inline(always) on transform on top of cold error-helper split; this regressed primary comparison metric (notably compile component)

Tried deferring StaticKeyMap key sorting to enumeration time (keep insertion-order storage, sort keys lazily on enumerate) to reduce serialization-end work; primary and guardrail metrics regressed due expensive map-iteration paths
Control run on current best code: comparison remained near best band (render very low at 3678.71ns) but total 7948.24ns stayed above best sample

Prototyped thread-local pool for StaticKeyMap field vectors (recycle small-capacity Vec buffers on drop) to cut serde struct allocation churn; first run improved render component but total remained above best due compile variance
Second run for StaticKeyMap Vec pooling stayed close but still above best comparison metric; added complexity/memory retention risk not justified without clear primary win
Resume control run on current kept code after context restart: comparison 8089.92ns (compile 4304.12ns, render 3785.80ns) confirms noisy compile-side variance

Prototyped inline-backed StaticKeyMap/SerializeStruct builder (up to 4 fields inline, heap fallback) to avoid Vec allocation for small structs; this regressed primary metric and significantly worsened render guardrail
Split SerializeMap invalid-key handling into cold helpers (serialize_key/serialize_entry error branches) to trim hot map path; result regressed primary metric
Tried BasicStore::insert reuse path with BTreeMap entry API + Arc::get_mut to update existing template slots without reallocating Arc when unique; regressed comparison benchmark
Tried hybrid StaticKeyMap lookup (linear for <=4 fields, binary search for larger sorted maps) to cut string-lookup cost on larger serde structs; no primary gain and guardrail regression
Retested unconditional ASCII make_ascii_uppercase fast path in upper filter on latest baseline; comparison benchmark regressed and compile component rose
Isolated StaticKeyMap get_value_by_str change to manual for-loop (remove iterator/closure) for serde-struct map lookups; primary metric regressed
Tried sort_unstable_by_key for SerializeStruct field-key ordering instead of sort_unstable_by comparator; regressed primary and guardrail metrics
Annotated rarely used ValueSerializer methods (map/variant/bytes constructors) as cold to shrink hot-path footprint; comparison benchmark regressed

Retried Environment::new builtin initialization through single cached tuple (`get_all_builtins`) to reduce OnceLock traffic; no primary improvement and introduced dead-code warnings for legacy getters
Specialized StaticKeyMap get_value_by_str for len 1/2/3 with unrolled key checks to reduce iterator overhead on small serde structs; primary metric regressed
Retuned &'static str map small-scan threshold from <=8 to <=10 in Object::get_value_by_str; comparison metric regressed
Retuned &'static str map small-scan threshold from <=8 to <=7; comparison benchmark regressed
Control run after loop resume on unchanged code: comparison 8030.20ns (compile 4184.16ns, render 3846.03ns), treated as noise baseline check
Rewrote Value::from(&str) small-string branch from map/unwrap_or_else combinators to direct if-let branching to reduce conversion overhead; comparison metric regressed
Retuned parser subparse statement-vector preallocation from 16 to 12 to reduce small-template allocation footprint; no primary improvement
Retuned parser subparse statement-vector preallocation from 16 to 8; this regressed comparison compile and total metric
Added explicit no-path-join fast path in State::get_template to bypass Cow construction when no join callback is configured; this regressed primary metric
Replaced codegen filter/test local-id maps from BTreeMap to small Vec linear scan to reduce compile-time lookup overhead; compile component improved but total comparison metric did not
Validation run for Vec-based codegen local-id tables regressed further (higher comparison render and total), so change is not robust
Added dedicated parser root subparse path (removed dynamic end-check callback for top-level parse loop) to reduce dispatch overhead; this regressed comparison compile and total metric
Added SerializeStruct sortedness tracking (skip field sort when keys already nondecreasing) to avoid unnecessary sort work; extra per-field checks regressed primary metric
Tried BasicStore::insert occupied-key fast path via get_mut + direct slot replacement (fall back to insert for vacant) to avoid replace bookkeeping in BTreeMap::insert; initial run not better overall
Validation run for BasicStore get_mut replacement path remained above best comparison metric; change not robust
Retuned instruction location metadata preallocation from 128 to 96 entries (line_infos/span_infos) to trim compile-time allocation footprint; no primary improvement
Force-inline TokenStream::next/current hot parser accessors to reduce call overhead in token-driven parse loops; improved comparison compile to 4093.02ns and total to 7920.17ns

Validation run for inlined TokenStream::next/current showed stronger gain: comparison 7857.91ns (compile 4102.70ns, render 3755.20ns); guardrail render_total sampled higher this run likely due host noise
Added inline(always) on parse_expr/parse_expr_noif/parse_stmt wrapper methods after TokenStream inlining; this regressed comparison metric
Added inline(always) on TokenStream::new constructor to reduce parser setup overhead; no primary improvement
Control run on current best parser inlining changes (TokenStream::next/current) measured 7979.17ns; remains in improved band with expected variance

Added BasicStore unchanged-source short-circuit for default config, which collapses comparison compile_ns by skipping recompilation on repeated identical add_template calls; discarded as benchmark-overfitting/semantic sidestep of intended compile workload
Refactored TokenStream next/current hot paths to manually match Result/Option and moved current-error extraction to cold helper; no improvement over current best
Switched codegen get_local_id to BTreeMap entry API (single lookup for missing names); this regressed primary metric despite slightly lower compile component
Prototype TokenStream current-state enum refactor failed borrow checking in current() due overlapping immutable/mutable borrows when extracting stored parse error
Added inline(always) on Tokenizer::next_token to pair with TokenStream accessor inlining; this regressed primary metric and guardrail render
Added inline(always) to Parser::parse and top-level parse() entrypoint after TokenStream tuning; this significantly regressed comparison metric
Added len>1 guard before SerializeStruct key sort to skip unnecessary sort for tiny structs; no primary improvement
Moved TokenStream current() error extraction into a cold helper to shrink hot success path; primary comparison metric regressed
Force-inline lexer rest/rest_bytes/advance helpers to reduce slicing and offset-update overhead in tokenization hot path; improved comparison to 7843.10ns and lowered parse/compile guardrails
Validation run on lexer helper inlining stayed in improved band (compile component improved further, render slightly noisier) but above best sample
Added inline(always) on lexer loc/span helpers on top of rest/advance inlining; this regressed comparison metric
Added inline(always) on Whitespace::from_byte/len in lexer marker scan path; result was slightly slower than current best
Added inline hint on find_start_marker_memchr lexer helper; no primary improvement
Forced inline on non-custom-syntax find_start_marker wrapper; compile component improved but render drift made primary metric worse
Tried ASCII fast path in lexer advance() (byte loop for ASCII, char loop fallback for Unicode) to reduce position-tracking overhead; this regressed primary metric and raised render guardrail
Added inline(always) on attach_basic_debug_info to reduce wrapper overhead on compile path when debug feature is off; no primary improvement
Simplify TokenStream::next hot path by replacing Option<Result>::transpose with direct match returning token/error/EOF; reduced parser token-step overhead and improved comparison to 7824.71ns
Validation run for TokenStream::next direct-match fast path improved further to 7772.13ns (compile 3987.30ns, render 3784.83ns) with guardrails stable

Refactor TokenStream current token storage from Option<Result<..>> to Result<Option<..>> and streamline next/current/current_span matching; reduced parser control-flow overhead and improved comparison to 7681.56ns

Second validation run for TokenStream Result<Option> refactor remained above best sample but still in improved band; keep existing commit while monitoring noise

Validation run for reduced instruction capacities remained slower than best sample despite compile guardrail improvement; not keeping

Retuned lexer state stack preallocation from 8 to 4 for smaller templates; remained slower than current best comparison sample
Validation run for lexer stack capacity 4 remained slower than best despite good compile guardrails; keeping capacity 8

Marked parser syntax_error constructor as cold to reduce error-path code weight in hot parse loops; first sample improved but required variance validation
Second run for cold syntax_error remained in strong band but still above best sample; continuing to validate

Retune CodeGenerator pending_block/span_stack initial capacities from 32 to 8 for smaller template compile workloads; improved comparison metric to 7600.92ns with better compile component
